turns.content: what are some good shows to binge during the day? I need something that's gonna keep me awake but also not too intense

Here are some good options depending on what you're in the mood for:

**Light but engaging:**
- *The Bear* — restaurant drama, great characters, short episodes
- *Abbott Elementary* — workplace comedy, really funny and heartwarming
- *Only Murders in the Building* — mystery comedy, easy to binge

**Slightly more involved but not heavy:**
- *Beef* — dark comedy, only 10 episodes
- *Shrinking* — comedy about a therapist, surprisingly touching

What kind of genres do you usually lean toward?
omg I watched The Bear and literally could not stop. that show is SO good. I already finished Abbott too, love Quinta. I haven't seen Shrinking though whats that about?

Shrinking is on Apple TV+ — it stars Jason Segel as a therapist who starts telling his patients exactly what he thinks instead of doing proper therapy. Harrison Ford is in it too and he's hilarious. It's funny but also has a lot of heart. If you liked Abbott Elementary's vibe of funny-but-also-makes-you-feel-things, you'd probably love it.
oh Harrison Ford being funny?? ok I'm intrigued. I do have Apple TV I think it came with my phone or something. what about reality shows because sometimes I just need to turn my brain completely off

For brain-off reality TV:

- *Love is Blind* — messy relationship drama, very bingeable
- *The Traitors* — like a murder mystery party game, surprisingly addictive
- *The Circle* — social media competition, fun and light
- *Selling Sunset* — real estate and drama, pure guilty pleasure
- *The Amazing Race* — if you want something with less drama and more adventure

Love is Blind is probably the most addictive of the bunch.
ok I watched Love is Blind seasons 1-4 and honestly after season 2 it went downhill but I could not stop watching lol. the Traitors I keep hearing about, is that the one with Alan Cumming?

Yes! Alan Cumming hosts it and he is absolutely perfect for it. He's so dramatic and campy. It's basically a group of celebrities and regular people in a castle in Scotland, and some are secretly "traitors" who eliminate people at night while the "faithfuls" try to figure out who they are. Think Mafia/Werewolf but with production value. It's really fun to watch and try to figure out who's lying.
ok you sold me on that one. a castle in Scotland AND Alan Cumming being dramatic? say less. I'm adding it to my list right now

You're going to love it. Season 1 is great for getting hooked, and Season 2 has even more chaos. Perfect daytime watching — engaging enough to keep you up but not so heavy you need to emotionally recover afterward. Enjoy!
lol exactly what I need. oh also do you know if there's a way to set like a sleep timer on Netflix? because sometimes I'll fall asleep watching something and wake up 4 episodes later completely lost

Yes! On most devices you can set a sleep timer. On your phone, use the built-in clock/timer app and set it to stop media when the timer goes off. On a TV, most smart TVs have a sleep timer in settings. Netflix also has a built-in "Are you still watching?" prompt that kicks in after a few episodes, but that's not super reliable if you're already out. The phone timer trick is the most consistent.

oh smart I didn't even think of using the phone timer for that. ok good because I've definitely woken up to some wild episode of something I didn't even start watching lol. ok one more thing — any good docuseries? like true crime or nature stuff? sometimes I need something shorter to switch to

For true crime:
- *The Staircase* — classic, very well-made
- *Bad Vegan* — wild story about a restaurant owner in NYC, only 4 episodes
- *The Tinder Swindler* — technically a doc film but SO bingeable

For nature/chill vibes:
- *Our Planet* on Netflix — David Attenborough narrating gorgeous footage
- *Night on Earth* — animals at night, really cool cinematography

Bad Vegan is probably the easiest to start with — it's short and absolutely bonkers.

ooh I actually watched the Tinder Swindler and my jaw was on the FLOOR the entire time. Bad Vegan sounds right up my alley though, 4 episodes is perfect for when I only have one day off. ok NOW I really have my list thank you so much lol I'm set for like a month of daytime TV

You've got a solid lineup now! Between The Traitors, Shrinking, and Bad Vegan, you're covered for every mood. Enjoy your daytime binge sessions — and try not to stay up watching "just one more episode" when you should be sleeping!
